basicDetails.schemeName: Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM): Self-Help Group - Uttarakhand

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The “Self-Help Group” is an initiative under the Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM), implemented by the Nagar Nigam Dehradun under the Department of Urban Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to empower urban poor women in Uttarakhand by promoting the formation of self-help groups, improving their livelihoods, and supporting them in income-generating and self-employment activities through financial incentives, bank loans, and government subsidies.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - In urban local body areas, urban poor women are provided with benefits under the scheme. - A one-time amount of ₹10,000/- is given to each group as an incentive by the Government of India. - Through bank loans, various activities are carried out by the groups to improve women’s livelihoods. - To encourage groups to adopt self-employment, subsidies are provided by both the Government of India and the State Government.
schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme aims to empower urban poor women in Uttarakhand by promoting the formation of self-help groups, improving their livelihoods, and supporting them in income-generating and self-employment activities through financial incentives, bank loans, and government subsidies.

children.text: The “Self-Help Group” is an initiative under the Deen Dayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NULM), implemented by the Nagar Nigam Dehradun under the Department of Urban Development, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to empower urban poor women in Uttarakhand by promoting the formation of self-help groups, improving their livelihoods, and supporting them in income-generating and self-employment activities through financial incentives, bank loans, and government subsidies.
In urban local body areas, urban poor women are provided with benefits under the scheme.
A one-time amount of ₹10,000/- is given to each group as an incentive by the Government of India.
Through bank loans, various activities are carried out by the groups to improve women’s livelihoods.
To encourage groups to adopt self-employment, subsidies are provided by both the Government of India and the State Government.

To form self-help groups, municipal employees involved in the project visit wards and hold group meetings with women. After the meetings, they encourage women to form groups and a self-help group is then formed.

After formation of the group, a savings account is opened for the group in any nationalized bank, into which the women of the group deposit their monthly savings. The women in the group are encouraged to take up self-employment. Actions for self-employment are carried out at the urban local body level through banks under the CCL (Cash Credit Limit) facility.

applicationProcess.process_md: To form self-help groups, municipal employees involved in the project visit wards and hold group meetings with women. After the meetings, they encourage women to form groups and a self-help group is then formed. After formation of the group, a savings account is opened for the group in any nationalized bank, into which the women of the group deposit their monthly savings. The women in the group are encouraged to take up self-employment. Actions for self-employment are carried out at the urban local body level through banks under the CCL (Cash Credit Limit) facility.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: 1. The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand. 1. The beneficiary must be a woman. 1. To form a Self-Help Group, the beneficiary must belong to an urban body. 1. The beneficiary must have an Aadhaar card & one passport-size photograph. 1. The annual income of the beneficiary's family must be less than ₹3.00 lakh.

children.text: The beneficiary must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
The beneficiary must be a woman.
To form a Self-Help Group, the beneficiary must belong to an urban body.
The beneficiary must have an Aadhaar card & one passport-size photograph.
The annual income of the beneficiary's family must be less than ₹3.00 lakh.
